1.notes: Opening par 4 at Muirfield Village. Slight downhill tee shot to generous fairway. Nicklaus design rewards smart positioning.

2.notes: Challenging uphill par 4. Creek crosses in front of green. Precision required on approach.

3.notes: Dogleg left par 4. Water guards left side. Play center-right off tee.

4.notes: Stunning downhill par 3 over water. One of Nicklaus's signature holes. Club selection critical.

5.notes: Reachable par 5 with risk-reward second shot. Creek guards left side of green.

6.notes: Strong par 4 with water left. Play safe to center-right of fairway.

7.notes: Long par 5, slight dogleg right. Water in play on layup and approach. Strategic three-shot hole.

8.notes: Short par 3 to elevated green. Bunkers surround putting surface. Back pin is treacherous.

9.notes: Closing hole of front nine. Water guards left. Strong finish required.

10.notes: Demanding start to back nine. Water left, bunkers right. Precision essential.

11.notes: Long par 5 with dramatic elevation change. Creek crosses twice. Birdie opportunity for long hitters.

12.notes: Iconic par 3 over water. Similar to Augusta's 12th. Wind swirls make club selection difficult.

13.notes: Demanding par 4 with water right. Favor left side off tee. Approach to elevated green.

14.notes: Short par 4 with big-hitting opportunity. Water guards right side of green. Precision rewarded.

15.notes: Reachable par 5 with significant risk. Water guards entire left side. Bold play can yield eagle.

16.notes: Dramatic par 3 over water to peninsula green. One of golf's most photographed holes.

17.notes: Demanding par 4 with water right. Green slopes severely. Birdie or bogey hole.

18.notes: Dramatic finishing hole. Creek crosses fairway and guards green. Theater for champions.

1.notes: Opening par 5 at Hogan's Alley. Trinity River runs right. Wide landing area but narrow approach.

2.notes: Narrow par 4. Accuracy off tee essential. Classic Perry Maxwell bunkering.

3.notes: Start of 'Horrible Horseshoe'. Long par 4 dogleg left. Demanding approach to well-bunkered green.

4.notes: Long par 3 in Horseshoe. Heavily bunkered. One of Hogan's favorite holes.

5.notes: Famous finishing hole of Horseshoe. Trinity River entire right side. One of golf's great risk-reward holes.

6.notes: Demanding par 4 requiring accuracy. Tree-lined fairway. Classic Hogan-style hole.

7.notes: Long par 4 with subtle dogleg right. Requires two precise shots.

8.notes: Short par 3 demanding accuracy. Well-bunkered green. Pin position changes character.

9.notes: Closing hole of front nine. Medium par 4 requiring precision. Classic Colonial.

10.notes: Opening par 4 of back nine. Straightforward but tight. Tree-lined throughout.

11.notes: Longest hole at Colonial. Par 5 with dogleg left. Risk-reward second shot over water.

12.notes: Strong par 4 with subtle dogleg right. Bunkers guard landing area and green.

13.notes: Short par 3 demanding precision. Deep bunkers guard putting surface.

14.notes: Long par 4 dogleg left. One of Colonial's toughest holes. Requires two quality shots.

15.notes: Strong par 4 requiring precision. Tight fairway with heavy bunkering.

16.notes: Short par 3 over water. Wind can affect club selection significantly.

17.notes: Par 4 dogleg right with water threat. Position off tee is critical.

18.notes: Classic finishing hole at Hogan's Alley. Strong par 4 requiring quality approach to well-protected green.

1.notes: Opening par 4 at TPC River Highlands. Generous fairway to start but well-bunkered green.

2.notes: Short par 4 with risk-reward opportunity. Big hitters can drive close to green.

3.notes: Long par 4 requiring two precise shots. Pete Dye bunkering throughout.

4.notes: Long par 4 with water right. Challenging second shot to elevated green.

5.notes: Long par 3 demanding precision. Bunkers surround the green.

6.notes: Long par 5 with water left on second shot. Reachable for long hitters willing to take on risk.

7.notes: Long par 4 requiring two quality shots. Fairway bunkers at drive distance.

8.notes: Par 3 over water. Club selection crucial. Wind can significantly affect ball flight.

9.notes: Closing hole of front nine. Dogleg right with water threat. Finishing position matters.

10.notes: Opening hole of back nine. Long par 4 demanding accuracy. Water left of green.

11.notes: Short par 3 over water. Deceptive wind conditions. Pin position changes dramatically.

12.notes: Par 4 dogleg left. Requires two quality shots. Well-bunkered throughout.

13.notes: Par 5 with risk-reward opportunity. Water guards right side throughout. Reachable in two for big hitters.

14.notes: Strong par 4 requiring two quality shots. Bunkers guard landing area and green.

15.notes: Famous drivable par 4. Water left, sand right. One of PGA Tour's most exciting holes. Anything from 2 to 6 possible.

16.notes: Par 3 over water to narrow green. Forced carry over water. Wind affects every shot.

17.notes: Iconic par 4 with water running entire right side. Large lake threatens drive and approach. Championship-defining hole.

hazards.notes: Difficult position leaves long approach over water

18.notes: Perfect finishing hole. Allows aggressive birdie attempt. Wide fairway but well-bunkered green demands precision.
